cards.title: I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me
cards.body: A parent seeks advice about their adult son who maintains only weekly contact despite devoted parenting. An expert explores whether the parent's initial reluctance to have children may be creating pressure that pushes him away.

cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than their childless peers. The mental and emotional gymnastics of parenting appear to provide significant cognitive benefits.

cards.title: I kept fearing the worst on our family holiday
cards.body: A parent with anxiety learns valuable lessons about coping during a real family emergency while on vacation. The experience teaches that worry can be managed and that they're more resilient than expected.

cards.title: Oliver's mum was a narcissist and his dad avoidant
cards.body: Childhood relationship patterns shaped by parental dysfunction continue affecting adults into their own relationships. Learning what healthy relationships look like takes time and intentional work.

cards.title: The Dad Nobody Screens For
cards.body: A father's postpartum withdrawal and quietness went undetected while his partner received screening for postpartum anxiety. The article highlights the importance of recognizing paternal mental health challenges after birth.
